skills.title: Choosing a Chunking Framework
skills.description: Chunking quality depends as much on the framework as on the strategy itself.

skills.title: Contextual Chunk Headers
skills.description: Contextual chunk headers (CCH) enhance retrieval by prepending higher-level context (document title, section headers, summaries) to each chunk before embedding.

skills.title: Hierarchical Chunking
skills.description: Hierarchical chunking creates multi-level chunk structures that preserve document hierarchies (chapters, sections, subsections).

skills.title: Semantic Chunking
skills.description: Semantic chunking divides documents into segments based on natural language boundaries and semantic meaning rather than fixed character counts.

skills.title: Chunking
skills.description: Use this parent skill when the main RAG problem is how to split source material into retrievable units.

skills.title: Sliding Window Chunking
skills.description: Sliding window chunking creates overlapping chunks where each chunk shares content with adjacent chunks.

skills.title: RAG for Code Documentation
skills.description: RAG for code documentation requires specialized handling due to code's structured nature, syntax-specific patterns, and the importance of preserving function signatures, imports, and contextual relationships.

skills.title: RAG for Multimodal Content
skills.description: Multimodal RAG extends retrieval to include images, videos, audio, and mixed media content alongside text.

skills.title: Data Type Handling
skills.description: Use this parent skill when source material is not plain prose or when different data types need different parsing, metadata, chunking, and retrieval strategies.

skills.title: Index Versioning and Reindexing
skills.description: This skill covers evolving the index behind a live RAG system: rebuilding embeddings after a model or chunking change, migrating between embedding models, and versioning indexes so changes are reversible and queries never go dark.

skills.title: Production RAG Deployment
skills.description: This skill covers turning a working RAG pipeline into a reliable production service: exposing it behind an API, scaling retrieval and generation independently, caching hot paths, and rolling out changes without breaking users.

skills.title: Deployment
skills.description: Use this parent skill when the RAG system works in development but must be made reliable in production.

skills.title: Generation Evaluation Metrics
skills.description: Generation evaluation measures the answer itself: whether it is grounded in the retrieved context, addresses the question, and is factually correct.

skills.title: RAG Evaluation Frameworks
skills.description: This skill covers the tooling layer of RAG evaluation: which framework to adopt, how to structure a golden set, and how to run evaluation automatically on every change so quality regressions are blocked before they ship.

skills.title: Retrieval Evaluation Metrics
skills.description: Retrieval evaluation measures whether the pipeline fetched the right chunks before the LLM ever sees them.

skills.title: Evaluation Metrics
skills.description: Use this parent skill when the main RAG problem is measuring quality, detecting regressions, or proving that a change actually helped.

skills.title: Optimize Retrieval Latency
skills.description: Optimizing RAG retrieval latency is critical for production applications where user experience depends on fast response times.

skills.title: Performance Optimization
skills.description: Use this parent skill when the RAG system works functionally but is too slow, expensive, or unstable under expected traffic.

skills.title: Agentic RAG Routing
skills.description: Agentic routing puts a decision step in front of retrieval: the agent classifies the query and chooses whether to retrieve, which source or index to hit, and which tool to call.

skills.title: Multi-Agent RAG Orchestration
skills.description: Multi-agent orchestration splits a complex question across specialized agents, such as a planner, one or more retrievers, and a synthesizer.

skills.title: RAG Agents
skills.description: Use this parent skill when a single retrieve-then-generate pass is not enough and the system must decide what to retrieve, which tools to call, or how to split work across agents.

skills.title: Adaptive Retrieval
skills.description: Adaptive retrieval classifies queries into types (factual, analytical, opinion, contextual) and applies different retrieval strategies optimized for each type.

skills.title: Context Enrichment Window
skills.description: Context enrichment window expands retrieved chunks by including neighboring text from the original document.

skills.title: CRAG - Corrective RAG
skills.description: Corrective RAG (CRAG) extends standard retrieval by dynamically evaluating document relevance and correcting the retrieval process when needed.

skills.title: Explainable Retrieval with Citations
skills.description: Explainable retrieval adds citations, source attribution, and traceability to RAG systems.

skills.title: Graph RAG - Knowledge Graph Retrieval
skills.description: Graph RAG enhances traditional retrieval by constructing knowledge graphs from documents, identifying communities of related entities, and using these structures to improve retrieval.

skills.title: Hybrid Search: BM25 + Dense
skills.description: Hybrid search combines BM25 (keyword search) with dense vector embeddings (semantic search) to leverage both exact term matching and semantic understanding.

skills.title: HyDE - Hypothetical Document Embeddings
skills.description: HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings) is a query expansion technique that generates a hypothetical document answering the user's query, then uses this synthetic document as the query for vector search.

skills.title: HyPE - Hypothetical Prompt Embeddings
skills.description: HyPE (Hypothetical Prompt Embeddings) transforms retrieval from query-document matching to question-question matching by generating multiple hypothetical questions for each document chunk during the indexing phase.

skills.title: Multi-Pass Retrieval with Reranking
skills.description: Multi-pass retrieval with reranking is a two-stage approach that first retrieves a broad set of candidates using fast bi-encoder search, then refines them using a more accurate but slower cross-encoder reranker.

skills.title: Query Transformation Strategies
skills.description: Query transformation strategies modify or expand user queries before retrieval to bridge the gap between natural language queries and document representations.

skills.title: RAPTOR - Hierarchical Abstractive Retrieval
skills.description: RAPTOR (Recursive Abstractive Processing and Tree-Organized Retrieval) creates a hierarchical tree of document summaries, allowing retrieval at multiple levels of abstraction.

skills.title: Self-RAG - Self-Reflective Retrieval
skills.description: Self-RAG is a reflective framework that decides whether to retrieve information, evaluates the relevance of retrieved documents, assesses response support, and rates output utility.

skills.title: Retrieval Strategies
skills.description: Use this parent skill when the main RAG problem is search quality, ranking, recall, context selection, or evidence traceability.

skills.title: Choosing Vector Database by Data Type
skills.description: Selecting the right vector database depends heavily on your data type (text, images, code, multimodal) and use case requirements.

skills.title: Qdrant for Production RAG
skills.description: Productionizing a RAG system with Qdrant requires considerations beyond basic setup: horizontal scaling, high availability, performance optimization, monitoring, and cost management.

skills.title: Qdrant Setup for RAG
skills.description: Qdrant is an open-source vector similarity search engine designed for high-performance RAG applications.

skills.title: Vector Databases
skills.description: Use this parent skill when the main RAG problem is choosing, configuring, or operating the vector storage layer.
